map.attribution: Map of Middle-earth by Peter Fenďa, CC BY-SA 3.0

characters.name: Frodo Baggins
characters.description: The Ring-bearer, a Hobbit of the Shire

characters.name: Aragorn
characters.description: Ranger of the North, heir of Isildur

characters.name: Gandalf
characters.description: Wizard of the Order of Istari

locations.name: The Shire
locations.realWorldNote: The Shire is modeled on the English Midlands countryside, particularly the area around Birmingham and Worcestershire. Tolkien explicitly stated this in letters.

locations.name: Bree
locations.realWorldNote: A crossroads town in Eriador. Located at the intersection of the East-West Road and the Greenway, approximately at map pixel [305, 335].

locations.name: Rivendell
locations.realWorldNote: The Last Homely House, located in a hidden valley in the Misty Mountains. Tolkien said it was partly inspired by Lauterbrunnen valley in Switzerland.

locations.name: Mines of Moria
locations.realWorldNote: The great underground realm of the Dwarves (Khazad-dûm) beneath the Misty Mountains.

locations.name: Lothlórien
locations.realWorldNote: The elvish realm of Galadriel and Celeborn in the angle between the rivers Celebrant and Anduin.

locations.name: Rohan (Edoras)
locations.realWorldNote: The kingdom of the Rohirrim. Edoras, the capital, sits on a hill at the foot of the White Mountains. Its culture and language are modeled on Anglo-Saxon England.

locations.name: Minas Tirith
locations.realWorldNote: The capital of Gondor, a seven-tiered city built into the face of Mount Mindolluin. Visually inspired by various Mediterranean and Byzantine fortified cities.

locations.name: Mordor (Mount Doom)
locations.realWorldNote: The dark land of Sauron, east of Gondor behind the Ephel Dúath mountain range. Mount Doom (Orodruin) is where the One Ring must be destroyed.

locations.name: Isengard
locations.realWorldNote: The stronghold of Saruman in a circular valley surrounded by a ring of mountains called the Nan Curunír.

events.quote: When Mr. Bilbo Baggins of Bag End announced that he would shortly be celebrating his eleventy-first birthday with a party of special magnificence, there was much talk and excitement in Hobbiton.
events.quoteContext: Opening of the novel, Frodo at home in the Shire.

events.quote: Gandalf! If you mean the wandering Wizard who gave Old Took a pair of magic diamond studs that fastened themselves and never came undone till ordered, I have not seen him for ages.
events.quoteContext: Gandalf arrives in the Shire for Bilbo's party.

events.chapterTitle: At the Sign of The Prancing Pony
events.quote: Bree was the chief village of the Bree-land, a small inhabited region, like an island in the empty lands round about.
events.quoteContext: Frodo and the hobbits arrive at Bree after leaving the Shire.

events.chapterTitle: At the Sign of The Prancing Pony
events.quote: In the corner they saw a weather-beaten man, rather dark, with a shaggy head of dark hair flecked with grey, and in his eyes a light like the gleam of water in a deep well.
events.quoteContext: Aragorn (as Strider) is first encountered at Bree's inn.

events.quote: When he came to himself again, he was still in the Hall of Fire, and it was still night. He was lying wrapped in blankets on a couch by the wall. Sam was sitting beside him.
events.quoteContext: Frodo recovers from his wound at Rivendell.

events.quote: Gandalf was there. His chair was at Frodo's side, and the table between them was littered with many papers.
events.quoteContext: Gandalf is present at Rivendell during the Council of Elrond.

events.quote: Strider turned and there was a fire in his eyes. It was Aragorn son of Arathorn, and he was smiling.
events.quoteContext: Aragorn's true identity is revealed at Rivendell.

events.quote: Frodo turned and fled, and all the Company turned with him. The orcs poured out of the doorway... it was Moria that had taken Gandalf.
events.quoteContext: The Fellowship passes through Moria; Gandalf falls at the Bridge.

events.quote: 'You cannot pass,' he said. The orcs stood still, and a dead silence fell. 'I am a servant of the Secret Fire, wielder of the flame of Anor.'
events.quoteContext: Gandalf confronts the Balrog at the Bridge of Khazad-dûm.

events.quote: 'Welcome, Frodo of the Shire,' she said. 'One member of the Company that set out from Rivendell is absent — where is Gandalf? For I look for him also.'
events.quoteContext: Frodo arrives in Lothlórien and meets Galadriel.

events.chapterTitle: The King of the Golden Hall
events.quote: So they passed through the outer courts and came before the gates of Meduseld; and their horses were taken by men of the King's household.
events.quoteContext: Aragorn arrives at Edoras, the hall of King Théoden of Rohan.

events.quote: They rode at last into the ravine of Nan Curunír. Night came dark under the mountains, and Isengard lay about them.
events.quoteContext: Aragorn and the Three Hunters ride to Isengard.

events.quote: Pippin looked out from the shelter of Gandalf's cloak. He wondered if he was awake or still sleeping, still in the swift-moving dream in which he had been wrapped so long since the great ride began.
events.quoteContext: The company arrives at Minas Tirith to prepare for the siege.

events.quote: Gandalf came and stood over him. 'Do you think,' said he, 'that the Lord of the Mark has no army? But Théoden's men have not yet come to the battle.'
events.quoteContext: Gandalf defends Minas Tirith during the siege.

events.quote: 'I am glad you are here with me,' said Frodo. 'Here at the end of all things, Sam.'
events.quoteContext: Frodo and Sam reach Mount Doom in Mordor.
